In my estimation the issue here involved is more correctly determined upon the ba*1005sis of issue preclusion as embodied in the doctrine of collateral estoppel. The doctrine of collateral estoppel is closely related to res judicata and has been said to constitute a “branch” of res judicata. But issue preclusion is not identical to claim preclusion. Wedel v. Unfortunately, that is what this court has done in this case. It has chosen to focus on the last part of the first sentence of paragraph 13 of the settlement agreement. I agree that that part of the sentence suggests that CCR member companies are severally liable. MOTION by Defendant Union Carbide Corporationin limine To Preclude Dr. Carl Brodkin From Testifying That Exposure To Union Carbide Asbestos Was A Cause In Fact Or A "Substantial Factor" In Causing Plaintiff's Mesothelioma (Bulger, Richard) (Entered: 08/01/2013) MINUTE entry before the Honorable Joan B. Gottschall: On January 22, 2015, the court addressed Lorillard Tobacco Company ("Lorillard") and Hollingsworth & Vose Companys ("H&V") motion in limine to exclude evidence of, or reference to, a study by Dr. James Talcott 296 . The court ordered (a) Lorillard and H&V to inform the court whether they would stipulate to the fact that crocidolite asbestos , as was used in the filter for original Kent cigarettes, is a potent amphibole type of asbestos that, in sufficient doses, can cause mesothelioma ; and (b) Plaintiff to explain the relevance of the Talcott study if Lorillard and H&V so stipulate. Plaintiff's and Lorillard and H&V's responses were due January 26, 2015. Lorillard and H&V submitted a stipulation in accord with the court's order. Plaintiff did not respond. Consequently, the court enters Lorillard and H&V's stipulation and Plaintiff may not introduce evidence of or reference the Talcott study at trial. Mailed notice (mjc, ) (Entered: 02/05/2015) MOTION by Defendant Georgia-Pacific LLC to join Defendant Union Carbide Corporations Motion In Limine To Preclude Dr. Carl Brodkin From Testifying That Exposure To Union Carbide Asbestos Was A Cause In Fact Or A Substantial Factor In Causing Plaintiffs Mesothelioma (Drumke, Michael) (Entered: 08/01/2013)
